task_completion.description: The agent opens the Java project or relevant Java files in an editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing an IDE (like IntelliJ) or text editor (like VS Code, nano, vim) open with the path-issues project files (e.g., Main.java, ConfigLoader.java).

task_completion.description: The agent removes the hardcoded Windows absolute path for the input CSV.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The code editor shows the input path changed from 'C:\Users\Dev\Documents\path-issues\data\inventory.csv' to a relative, cross-platform path like 'data/inventory.csv' or using File.separator.

task_completion.description: The agent replaces Windows backslash path separators with cross-platform separators.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The code editor shows the output path changed from 'output\report.txt' to 'output/report.txt' or using File.separator / Paths.get.

task_completion.description: The agent corrects the case-sensitivity error in the config filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The code editor shows the config filename changed from 'Config.properties' to 'config.properties' (lowercase 'c').

task_completion.description: The agent compiles and runs the refactored Java application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal or IDE run tool window showing the Java/Maven execution completing successfully without FileNotFoundException or path errors.

task_completion.description: The agent verifies the generated report contains the correct inventory count.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal or editor showing the contents of 'output/report.txt' containing the text 'Total Items: 50'.

integrity.description: The agent actually modified the Java source code files rather than just creating the output file manually.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent editing .java files in an editor, not just using terminal commands like 'echo' to create the report.txt file.

integrity.description: The output report was generated by running the Java application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal or IDE should show the execution command (e.g., mvn exec:java or running via IDE run button) immediately preceding the creation or checking of the output file.

integrity.description: The agent did not hardcode the expected output string directly into the Java code to bypass reading the CSV.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Java code should still contain logic to read lines from the file and calculate the size, rather than a hardcoded 'writer.write("Total Items: 50")'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project is located at /home/ga/IdeaProjects/path-issues. The original broken code contains the Windows absolute path 'C:\Users\Dev\Documents\path-issues\data\inventory.csv', backslash separators like 'output\report.txt', and the incorrect config filename 'Config.properties'. The corrected code must use relative paths (e.g., 'data/inventory.csv', 'output/report.txt') and the lowercase 'config.properties'. The application must successfully run and generate 'output/report.txt' containing exactly 'Total Items: 50'.
